# resolve(mergeConflicts:)

**Instance Method**

Resolves the conflicts in a given list.

## Declaration

```swift
func resolve(mergeConflicts list: [Any]) throws
```

## Parameters

- **list**: An array of merge conflicts (instances of [doc://com.apple.coredata/documentation/CoreData/NSMergeConflict]).

## Discussion

If you override this method in a subclass, you should typically invoke the superclass’s implementation in addition to performing your own operations.

## Resolving a Conflict

- **resolve(constraintConflicts:)**: Resolves the conflicts in a given list.
- **resolve(optimisticLockingConflicts:)**: Resolves the conflicts in a given list.
